tier1.label: Institutional
tier1.description: Peer-reviewed publications, government data, established academic or scientific bodies.

tier2.label: Reputable
tier2.description: Established journalism and named subject-matter experts with documented credentials.

notes.wsj.com: Opinion section should be treated as Tier 3 for factual claims.
notes.nytimes.com: Opinion section should be treated as Tier 3 for factual claims.

communityVerified.label: Community-Verified
communityVerified.description: Community-edited. Surfaced as context with explicit labeling. Eligible as anchor for commentary layer.

tier3.label: Unrated
tier3.description: Discussion boards, social media, blogs, other wikis, anonymous sources. Never generate tension ratings. May surface as commentary layer only when anchored to Tier 1/2/community-verified source.

topicCaveats.description: Known reliability caveats for specific sources on specific topic areas.
